13	# Server Operator Workbench Plan
14	
15	## Goal
16	
17	Make `ddx server` the basic operator surface for DDx work:
18	
19	- see all workers across all local projects, and across all nodes when the
20	  current node is a federation hub
21	- see what every worker is doing now: project, bead, attempt, phase, route,
22	  elapsed time, and freshness
23	- start and stop more autonomous `ddx work` workers from the web UI
24	- mutate bead queues for any registered project
25	- edit project specs/documents, especially `docs/helix/**`
26	
27	This plan intentionally excludes Axon backend rollout and 2k-scale artifact
28	performance. The basic contract is JSONL-backed projects, existing server
29	worker launch, existing GraphQL/web stack, and owner-targeted federation writes.
30	
31	## Design Anchors
32	
33	- FEAT-021 US-100 through US-104 define the operator stories.
34	- FEAT-026 US-097b defines owner-targeted federation writes.
35	- ADR-022 remains load-bearing: workers are autonomous and the bead store is the
36	  claim authority. The server launches, observes, and requests stop; it does not
37	  become the correctness authority.
38	- ADR-007 remains load-bearing: hub/spoke federation never broadcasts writes.
39	  Every write targets exactly one owner node and one owner project.
40	- TP-002 TC-016 through TC-019 define the E2E contract for basic functionality.
41	
42	## E2E Test Plan
43	
44	1. `workers-single-pane.spec.ts`
45	   - Local fixture: two registered projects with reported and live workers.
46	   - Assert `/nodes/:nodeId/workers` merges rows with project badges, current
47	     bead, phase, route/model, elapsed time, and freshness.
48	   - Assert row detail shows prompt, output, recent events, attempt id, current
49	     bead link, and stop action.
50	   - Federation fixture: hub plus spoke. Assert `scope=federation` merges hub
51	     and spoke workers with node badges.
52	
53	2. `workers-dispatch.spec.ts`
54	   - Start two workers for one project through `startWorker`.
55	   - Assert both rows appear and the queue/worker summary updates.
56	   - Assert `workers.max_count` refusal is visible.
57	   - Stop one worker and assert terminal/stopping state plus audit event.
58	
59	3. `federation-worker-control.spec.ts`
60	   - Start hub and spoke.
61	   - From the hub, start a worker for a spoke project.
62	   - Assert exactly one worker starts on the spoke and appears in hub
63	     federation scope.
64	   - Stop the worker through the hub.
65	   - Simulate offline spoke and assert command refusal with no phantom row.
66	
67	4. `beads-queue-mutations.spec.ts`
68	   - Local multi-project fixture: create/edit/lifecycle-mutate a bead in
69	     project A and assert project B isolation.
70	   - Federation fixture: create/edit a bead in a spoke project from the hub and
71	     assert the spoke owns the mutation and the hub read model updates.
72	
73	5. `spec-editing.spec.ts`
74	   - Edit and save a `docs/helix/**` document in one project.
75	   - Assert path confinement rejects absolute and traversal writes.
76	   - Assert stale-write protection refuses overwrites.
77	   - Repeat save through hub-to-spoke forwarding.
78	
79	## Implementation Plan
80	
81	### Wave 1: Local Worker Workbench
82	
83	Build the local node-wide and project-scoped worker views around existing
84	`reportedWorkers`, `workersByProject`, `queueAndWorkersSummary`, `startWorker`,
85	`stopWorker`, and `workerProgress` surfaces. The UI should prefer server-derived
86	reported worker state and fall back to polling where WebSocket/subscription
87	streams are unavailable.
88	
89	### Wave 2: Local Worker Dispatch Hardening
90	
91	Make `startWorker` accept count/mode/filter inputs safely, enforce
92	`workers.max_count`, return one result per started worker, and emit audit events.
93	Keep each worker autonomous. Launch failures must surface as typed UI errors,
94	not silent rows.
95	
96	### Wave 3: Owner-Targeted Federation Writes
97	
98	Add forwarding for `startWorker`, `stopWorker`, bead mutations, and
99	`documentWrite`. Each forwarded command carries origin identity, forwarding path,
100	request id, target node/project, and expected version where needed. The owner
101	executes locally or rejects. The hub never creates local fallback state for a
102	spoke write.
103	
104	### Wave 4: Queue and Spec Mutation UX
105	
106	Complete the bead editor/lifecycle actions and spec editor flows for local and
107	federated projects. Use path confinement and stale-write protection for document
108	writes. Show mutation errors inline and refresh the affected lists/details after
109	success.
110	
111	### Wave 5: Reliability Gate
112	
113	Wire the new E2E specs into the functional gate. Keep scale/perf and Axon
114	coverage separate so basic operator functionality can go green independently.
115	
116	## Non-Scope
117	
118	- Axon backend implementation or Axon subscriptions.
119	- 2k artifact fixture performance and FEAT-008 budget locking.
120	- Managed-node outbound command channel from FEAT-029/ADR-028.
121	- Distributed consensus, replication, or hub failover.
122	- Changing the bead store claim authority from project-local stores to the
123	  server.
124	
125	## Bead Breakdown
126	
127	The implementation beads for this plan should carry:
128	
129	- `spec-id=PLAN-2026-06-08-SERVER-OPERATOR-WORKBENCH`

133	  go test ...`, the relevant `bunx playwright test ...` command, and `lefthook
134	  run pre-commit`
135

129	
130	All DDx run records across all registered projects, newest first. The project
131	each record belongs to is shown inline, and records retain their FEAT-010 layer
132	label: `work`, `try`, or `run`.

138	- Time range filter
139	- Click to expand: DDx metadata, native session references, token usage,
140	  parent/child links, and artifact producer links
141	- Layer-aware row expansion replaces the retired Sessions and Executions
142	  top-level activity tabs and opens the shared run-detail tab strip:
143	  `layer=work` rows expose `overview` only plus queue inputs and child run
144	  links; `layer=try` rows expose `overview`, `prompt`, `response`, `tools`,
145	  and `evidence`; `layer=run` rows expose `overview`, `prompt`, `response`,
146	  `session`, `tools`, and `evidence`.
147	- Re-queue affordances per FEAT-008 US-086c (try-layer + run-layer
148	  re-queue button; work-layer "Start worker from this drain"); every
149	  successful re-queue emits a `run_requeue` audit event on the
150	  originating bead per FEAT-010 §"Re-queue audit event schema".
151	- Same capabilities as FEAT-008 US-086 and US-086b

157	### Combined Worker Workbench (`/nodes/:nodeId/workers`)
158	
159	All DDx work workers across the selected node's registered projects merged into
160	one operator view. When the node is a federation hub, the view accepts
161	`?scope=federation` and includes workers reported by registered spokes. Each row
162	shows the owner node, project, worker id, state, current bead id/title when
163	available, attempt id, phase, harness/provider/model, elapsed time, last event
164	time, freshness (`connected`, `stale`, `disconnected`, `offline`), and the
165	worker command mode (`once`, `drain`, or `watch`).
166	
167	- Scope toggle: `LOCAL` shows this node only; `FEDERATION` fans out across
168	  registered spokes when hub-mode is enabled.
169	- Project and node filters are URL-backed.
170	- Rows link to the owning project worker detail route.
171	- A detail drawer shows recent phase transitions, captured output, prompt,
172	  route/model, current bead, attempt bundle link, and stop/cancel actions.
173	- `+ Add worker` starts one or more autonomous `ddx work` workers for a selected
174	  project through the server. The server is a launcher and observer; the bead
175	  store remains the claim authority per ADR-022.
176	- Hub-mode worker start/stop commands are owner-targeted. The hub forwards to
177	  the node that owns the selected project; it never broadcasts a worker command.
178	
179	**GraphQL Queries:** `reportedWorkers`, `workersByProject`, `queueAndWorkersSummary`.
180

83		workersByProject: WorkerConnection
84		queueAndWorkersSummary: { maxCount: number | null }
85	}
86	
87	export interface ReportedWorker {
88		id: string
89		project: string
90		harness: string
91		state: string
92		lastEventAt: string
93		mirrorFailuresCount: number
94		hadDroppedBackfill: boolean
95		currentBead: string | null
96		currentAttempt: string | null
97	}
98	
99	interface ReportedWorkersResult {
100		reportedWorkers: ReportedWorker[]
101	}
102	
103	interface ProjectsApiEntry {
104		id: string
105		name?: string
106		path?: string
107	}
108	
109	async function fetchProjectPath(
110		fetchImpl: typeof globalThis.fetch,
111		projectId: string
112	): Promise<string | null> {
113		try {
114			const resp = await fetchImpl('/api/projects')
115			if (!resp.ok) return null
116			const list = (await resp.json()) as ProjectsApiEntry[]
117			const match = list.find((p) => p.id === projectId)
118			return match?.path ?? null
119		} catch {
120			return null
121		}
122	}
123	
124	export const load: LayoutLoad = async ({ params, fetch }) => {
125		const fetchImpl = fetch as unknown as typeof globalThis.fetch
126		const client = createClient(fetchImpl)
127	
128		const [data, reportedRaw, projectPath] = await Promise.all([
129			client.request<WorkersResult>(WORKERS_QUERY, { projectID: params.projectId }),
130			client
131				.request<ReportedWorkersResult>(REPORTED_WORKERS_QUERY)
132				.catch(() => ({ reportedWorkers: [] as ReportedWorker[] })),
133			fetchProjectPath(fetchImpl, params.projectId)
134		])
135	
136		const reportedAll = reportedRaw.reportedWorkers ?? []
137		const reported = projectPath
138			? reportedAll.filter((w) => w.project === projectPath)
139			: reportedAll
140	
141		return {
142			projectId: params.projectId,
143			projectPath,
144			workers: data.workersByProject,
145			maxCount: data.queueAndWorkersSummary?.maxCount ?? null,
146			reportedWorkers: reported
147		}
148	}

data.text: The `@(workers-pane)` layout reset doesn't skip the root layout in SvelteKit 2.x - the root layout always applies. The cleanest solution is to conditionally skip NavShell for the node-wide workers path in the root layout:

input.old_string:
<script lang="ts">
	import '../app.css';
	import favicon from '$lib/assets/favicon.svg';
	import { ModeWatcher } from 'mode-watcher';
	import CommandPalette from '$lib/components/CommandPalette.svelte';
	import NavShell from '$lib/components/NavShell.svelte';
	import { nodeStore } from '$lib/stores/node.svelte';

	let { data, children } = $props();

	$effect(() => {
		if (data.nodeInfo) nodeStore.set(data.nodeInfo);
	});
</script>

<ModeWatcher />
<svelte:head><link rel="icon" href={favicon} /></svelte:head>

<NavShell>
	{@render children()}
</NavShell>
<CommandPalette />
input.new_string:
<script lang="ts">
	import '../app.css';
	import favicon from '$lib/assets/favicon.svg';
	import { ModeWatcher } from 'mode-watcher';
	import CommandPalette from '$lib/components/CommandPalette.svelte';
	import NavShell from '$lib/components/NavShell.svelte';
	import { nodeStore } from '$lib/stores/node.svelte';
	import { page } from '$app/stores';

	let { data, children } = $props();

	$effect(() => {
		if (data.nodeInfo) nodeStore.set(data.nodeInfo);
	});

	// Node-wide workers workbench: full-screen layout without NavShell
	// so project-picker options don't conflict with worker table project badges.
	const isWorkersPanePath = $derived(/^\/nodes\/[^/]+\/workers(\/|$)/.test($page.url.pathname));
</script>

<ModeWatcher />
<svelte:head><link rel="icon" href={favicon} /></svelte:head>

{#if isWorkersPanePath}
	{@render children()}
{:else}
	<NavShell>
		{@render children()}
	</NavShell>
{/if}
<CommandPalette />
